name: Telegram
one_liner: Cloud-based messaging platform offering a REST Bot API and the lower-level MTProto client API for building bots, userbots, and messaging integrations.
primary_auth: Bot token (obtained from @BotFather) for the Bot API; api_id + api_hash plus user session auth for the MTProto client API.
access_notes: Bot tokens are issued instantly and free by messaging @BotFather in the app. MTProto api_id/api_hash are obtained free and self-serve at my.telegram.org (one per phone number). No payment or human approval required for either.
api_notes: Two APIs: the HTTP Bot API (100+ methods over HTTPS at api.telegram.org/bot<token>/METHOD, GET/POST) and the low-level MTProto client API (used via TDLib and libraries like Telethon/GramJS) which exposes the full user-account surface. Bot API covers messaging, media, inline queries, payments, games, and more.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server. Multiple community MCP servers exist (e.g. chigwell/telegram-mcp and sparfenyuk/mcp-telegram via Telethon/MTProto, chaindead/telegram-mcp) but none published by Telegram.
main_blocker: None significant; credentials are free and self-serve. Note the Bot API and MTProto are distinct surfaces, and MTProto usage is monitored for spam/abuse.

evidence.claim: Bot API uses a unique authentication token from BotFather, served over HTTPS at api.telegram.org/bot<token>/METHOD, supports GET/POST, and provides setWebhook for updates.
api_id and api_hash for the MTProto API are obtained free and self-serve by logging into my.telegram.org and using API development tools.
Telegram MCP servers are community-built (Telethon/MTProto based), with no official Telegram-published server.

open_questions: None material; both auth paths, free self-serve access, and API breadth are confirmed by official docs.
